task.title: Add review-map artifact for large changesets.

context.problem_statement: Large changesets need a reusable review-map artifact that turns a broad diff into navigable review clusters, sequence, risk hotspots, and omission notes for humans and agents.
background: AGW-032 added scripts/build_context_packet.py and make context-packet for deterministic changed-file adjacency: likely callers, likely tests, related docs, ADRs, scripts, and runtime configs.
The research feature matrix still records review-map artifacts for large changes as the remaining AGW-033 context-retrieval slice.
The backlog delivery shape is Markdown/JSON template, so this task should define artifact templates and schema contracts rather than shipping a new CLI command.
AGW-029 defined the source-side trace concept that composes receipts, task packets, context packets, task status, and closeout receipts without exposing private transcripts.
AGW-034 remains the likely place for LSP or codegraph lookup; AGW-033 should not implement that deeper analysis.
non_goals: Do not add a new command, Make target, hosted service, GitHub adapter, browser workflow, or network behavior.
Do not modify repo-contract-kit templates, installed target-repo files, or kit release behavior.
Do not rework scripts/build_context_packet.py unless a focused test proves the template cannot compose with its current output.
Do not implement AGW-034 LSP/codegraph lookup, semantic call graphing, or cross-language symbol indexing.
Do not change review-runner execution behavior or agent dispatch orchestration.
Do not mark AGW-033 done until the Markdown template, JSON schema, docs references, generated adapters, tests, and backlog closeout all agree.

previous_task_state.notes: AGW-033 packet creation began from a clean source checkout on main at 67476a373b7e4ebe885e125e787f359d44e1468c. make agent-next selected AGW-033 with dirty false, and make agent-task-status TASK_STATUS_JSON=1 reported zero active tasks, zero hazards, zero stale tasks, zero unknown-scope tasks, and zero untracked agent worktrees.

closeout_required_before_start.reason: AGW-031 is closed and pushed, the source checkout is clean, and the task-status receipt reports no active or stale work to finalize before AGW-033 starts.
closeout_required_before_start.required_next_step: Proceed with exactly one AGW-033 worker in agent-workflow-kit; refresh git status and task-status before launch and stop if new dirt, active task metadata, or remote divergence appears.
evidence_paths: make agent-next selected AGW-033 with dirty false

goal_alignment.repo_goal: Keep agent-workflow-kit as the canonical source for local-first review prompts, workflow templates, schemas, research artifacts, tests, and generated Codex adapters.

area_contracts.purpose: New canonical Markdown artifact template for mapping a broad changeset into review clusters, sequence, risk hotspots, and omissions.
New machine-readable contract for review-map artifacts so agents and tools can validate the same structure as the Markdown template.
Existing deterministic context packet source that AGW-033 should compose with, not replace.
Prompt index and workflow routing docs for reusable templates, deterministic reports, and output artifacts.
Harness artifact inventory and metric families that should name review maps as a context-relevance and review-navigation surface.
Adapter and skill-pack manifest; include the review-map template in the review skill if the worker adds it to the review workflow surface.

stop_conditions: Stop if the implementation requires repo-contract-kit installed behavior, release changes, or managed target files.
Stop if a review-map command or runner behavior is required; create a follow-on item instead.
Stop if LSP, codegraph, or semantic symbol lookup is needed to satisfy the template; leave that to AGW-034.
Stop if the Markdown template and JSON schema cannot be kept structurally aligned.
Stop if generated adapter checks show unrelated prompt drift outside the AGW-033 scope.
Stop if the final artifact encourages reviewers to skip direct inspection of source, tests, docs, ADRs, or runtime evidence.

protected_files: repo-contract-kit managed templates or installed target-repo files
scripts/build_context_packet.py unless a focused failing test proves a narrow composition bug
AGW-034 LSP or codegraph lookup surfaces
unrelated personas, unrelated backlog rows, unrelated task packets, and unrelated dirty work
expected_outputs: A Markdown review-map template under workflows/prompts/templates/ with sections for source inputs, scope summary, change clusters, entrypoints, contracts, risk hotspots, review order, validation surfaces, omissions, and follow-up packet candidates.
A JSON schema under schemas/ that represents the same review-map artifact with stable required fields and bounded optional fields.
Prompt/docs guidance that review maps compose with make context-packet and context bundles, and do not replace source inspection or evidence-backed findings.
Generated .codex prompt adapter files refreshed from workflows/prompts.
A focused test that validates the review-map schema and checks that the Markdown template names the required navigation and omission sections.
Manifest or skill-pack include updates only if needed so review users receive the template through the existing review prompt surface.
Version, changelog, docs, feature matrix, summary, and backlog closeout updates after validation.

acceptance_criteria.description: A reusable Markdown review-map template exists for large changesets and guides reviewers through changed-file clusters, entrypoints, contracts, risk hotspots, review sequence, validation surfaces, omissions, and follow-up packet candidates.
A machine-readable review-map JSON schema exists and mirrors the Markdown artifact structure without requiring private transcript data or hosted-service fields.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Inspect schemas/review-map.schema.json and run the focused schema/template test.

acceptance_criteria.description: The review-map guidance explicitly composes with make context-packet or installed context bundles and says when direct source, tests, docs, ADRs, scripts, runtime config, or receipts must still be inspected.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Inspect template and docs references in workflows/prompts/README.md, docs/using-the-prompt-kit.md, docs/harness-engineering.md, or docs/codex-review-trace.md as applicable.

acceptance_criteria.description: The artifact has explicit omission and uncertainty fields so reviewers can record skipped files, unclassified clusters, missing context-packet data, ambiguous ownership, and validation gaps.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Schema test and manual template review confirm required omission fields.

acceptance_criteria.description: The change stays source-side and does not add a CLI, Make target, repo-contract-kit behavior, LSP/codegraph lookup, or review-runner mutation.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run git diff --name-only and inspect changed files against the allowed/protected scope.

acceptance_criteria.description: Generated Codex adapters are current and any manifest/skill-pack include change is validated.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 make prompt-adapters-export, PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 make prompt-adapters-check, and the relevant adapter/skill-pack unittest if manifest changed.

acceptance_criteria.description: Docs impact, version, changelog, feature matrix, summary, and backlog closeout reflect that AGW-033 shipped a Markdown/JSON artifact contract, not a command.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Run docs checks, make version-check, make backlog-check, and make backlog-split-check after closeout.

commands.expected_result: pass after the focused review-map schema/template test is added
pass if workflows/manifest.json changed; otherwise record not applicable
pass and refresh generated prompt adapters
pass; pre-existing instruction hygiene warnings may be reported if unchanged
pass; pre-existing instruction hygiene warnings may be reported if unchanged
pass after a patch bump and changelog entry

evidence_to_capture: Diff summary and changed-file list proving source-side template/schema/docs scope.

readiness_check.command: make agent-task-ready TASK=AGW-033 TASK_READY_JSON=1 or record why unavailable in the source parent run
readiness_check.expected_result: ready report passes or a blocker is recorded before implementation starts

lifecycle_action.command: make agent-task-finalize TASK=AGW-033 TASK_RECEIPT=<path> TASK_FINALIZE_JSON=1 or lifecycle fallback
lifecycle_action.expected_result: metadata is closed and final receipt is linked, or fallback receipt is durable

final_task_status.expected_result: task is terminal or no local metadata was created and the reason is recorded

closeout_preview.expected_result: eligible, retained, or blocked cleanup state is recorded

closeout_requirements.dirty_state_explanation: Final handoff must say whether the checkout is clean, only expected files are dirty, cleanup is blocked, or unrelated dirt was preserved.

docs_impact.notes: This adds a user-facing review artifact contract and schema for large changesets.

known_risks: A review map can become a substitute for direct source inspection if the template does not require evidence and omissions.
A broad template can become too verbose, increasing token cost for large reviews unless sections are bounded and optional details are summarized.
Schema and Markdown template drift can undermine downstream validation if tests only check one side.
The task can expand into CLI, LSP, or repo-contract-kit behavior unless the worker preserves the Markdown/JSON artifact boundary.
If manifest skill includes are missed, generated skill-pack users may not receive the new template even though Codex adapters do.
stop_conditions: The implementation needs repo-contract-kit or installed target-repo changes.
The worker cannot keep the Markdown template and JSON schema structurally aligned.
Validation shows prompt adapters cannot be regenerated cleanly.
A proposed change adds review-runner, CLI, Make target, network, or LSP behavior.
The artifact omits explicit unknowns, skipped files, or validation gaps.

approval.notes: Active user goal is to add fully planned backlog packets and implement remaining workflow-stack items serially with one worker per backlog item.

token_budget.guidance: Use the inspect_first list and existing context-packet output before broad rereads. Keep the review-map template bounded enough for large-diff handoff.

handoff.recommended_prompt: workflows/prompts/task-packet.md, then workflows/prompts/fix-implementer.md for the approved AGW-033 source-side artifact slice
dependencies: AGW-032 context packet exists and should be composed with, not replaced.
AGW-029 trace concept exists and can mention review-map artifacts as a local evidence surface if docs are updated.
AGW-034 LSP/codegraph lookup remains a follow-on and must not be implemented here.
Generated .codex adapters must come from make prompt-adapters-export.
handoff.next_packet_hint: After AGW-033 closes, continue with the next open backlog item selected by make agent-next.
